hazard.summary: Intermittent HTTP 429 on Azure PrivateEndpoint creation; reduced parallelism (--parallel 1) mitigates, suggesting burst/concurrency exceeds provider/API budget even after retries.
signals: maintainer notes retries already exist; 429 still surfaces after backoff

constraints: Concurrency must be capped to stay within Azure/broker throttling limits for this resource/API path.
Retries must be bounded by a budget (attempts and/or max elapsed time) to avoid indefinite churn.
Backoff should respect server hints (Retry-After) and include jitter to prevent synchronized retry storms.
knobs: emit_retry_summary (attempts, elapsed, last Retry-After) in debug logs
verification: Repro: run a minimal program that creates PrivateEndpoint at parallelism N>1; observe frequency of 429.
Mitigation: set --parallel 1 (or provider-level concurrency cap) and confirm 429 disappears or drops sharply.
Instrumentation: confirm debug output reports retry attempts and total elapsed retry time when 429 occurs.
